sections.heading: What is a Size to Weight Calculator?
sections.content: A Size to Weight Calculator estimates the weight of a rectangular cuboid using its length, width, height, and material density. Ideal for construction, manufacturing, shipping, DIY projects, and education.

subSections.heading: Select Material
subSections.content: Choose a preset material (steel, wood, concrete, aluminum) or enter custom density.

subSections.heading: Select Units
subSections.content: Ensure units are consistent (cm, m, mm, in, ft).

subSections.heading: Click Calculate
subSections.content: Get the weight instantly in kg or lb.

subSections.heading: Tip
subSections.content: Verify density matches the actual material for accurate results.

subSections.heading: Flexible Units
subSections.content: Supports cm, m, mm, in, and ft.

subSections.heading: Preset & Custom Materials
subSections.content: Easy selection or custom density input.

subSections.heading: Construction & Engineering
subSections.content: Weight of beams, blocks, and components.

subSections.heading: DIY & Fabrication
subSections.content: Feasibility check for furniture, enclosures, or projects.

subSections.heading: Education
subSections.content: Learn volume, density, and mass relationships.

subSections.heading: Example 1 – Wood Block
subSections.content: 50 cm × 40 cm × 30 cm, Oak Wood (Density 750 kg/m³) → Weight ≈ 45 kg

subSections.heading: Example 2 – Steel Box
subSections.content: 2 m × 1 m × 0.5 m, Steel (Density 7850 kg/m³) → Weight ≈ 7850 kg

subSections.heading: Example 3 – Custom Material
subSections.content: 1 ft × 0.5 ft × 0.25 ft, Density 500 kg/m³ → Weight ≈ 1.75 kg

faq.q: What is a Size to Weight Calculator?
faq.a: It estimates cuboid weight using dimensions and material density.
Yes, cm, m, mm, inches, and feet are supported.
Yes, steel, wood, concrete, and aluminum.
Yes, enter any density for accurate calculation.

faq.q: Is it suitable for DIY projects?
faq.a: Yes, for furniture, enclosures, or other custom objects.

faq.q: Does it calculate in kg and lb?
faq.a: Yes, results are shown in both units.
Yes, works on smartphones, tablets, and desktops.
Engineers, builders, DIYers, students, and logistics planners.

faq.q: Does it account for hollow or irregular objects?
faq.a: No, it assumes a solid cuboid of uniform density.
Yes, completely free with no downloads required.
